questions.question: What does the particulate nature of matter state?
questions.answer: It states that matter is composed of extremely small particles.

questions.question: What happens when sugar dissolves in water?
questions.answer: Sugar particles spread uniformly among water particles.

questions.question: What does the dissolution of sugar in water indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates that particles of matter have spaces between them.

questions.question: What is meant by interparticle attraction?
questions.answer: It is the force that holds particles of matter together.

questions.question: Which state of matter has closely packed particles?
questions.answer: Solids.

questions.question: Why do liquids flow easily?
questions.answer: Because their particles can slide past one another.

questions.question: Why are gases easily compressible?
questions.answer: Because there are large spaces between gas particles.

questions.question: What property allows gases to fill the entire container?
questions.answer: Free movement of gas particles.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
questions.answer: The movement of particles from higher concentration to lower concentration.

questions.question: Why does diffusion occur faster in gases?
questions.answer: Because gas particles move faster and are farther apart.

questions.question: What happens to particle motion when temperature increases?
questions.answer: Particles gain energy and move faster.

questions.question: What process changes solid into liquid?
questions.answer: Melting.

questions.question: What happens to interparticle attraction during melting?
questions.answer: It becomes weaker.

questions.question: What is boiling point?
questions.answer: The temperature at which a liquid changes into gas.

questions.question: Why does evaporation occur from the surface of liquids?
questions.answer: Because surface particles gain enough energy to escape as vapor.

questions.question: Name one factor that increases evaporation.
questions.answer: High temperature.

questions.question: What are fluids?
questions.answer: Substances that can flow such as liquids and gases.

questions.question: Which state of matter has the fastest particle movement?
questions.answer: Gases.

questions.question: Why do solids generally have high density?
questions.answer: Because particles are tightly packed.

questions.question: Why does perfume smell spread in a room?
questions.answer: Due to diffusion of gas particles.

questions.question: What does the potassium permanganate experiment demonstrate?
questions.answer: Continuous movement of particles in liquids.

questions.question: What happens to particle spacing when matter changes from liquid to gas?
questions.answer: Particle spacing increases greatly.

questions.question: Which state of matter shows least compressibility?
questions.answer: Solids.

questions.question: What happens to particle energy when heat is supplied?
questions.answer: Particle kinetic energy increases.

questions.question: What does particle theory explain?
questions.answer: It explains the properties and behavior of matter based on particle arrangement and motion.
